---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "from_xenium_to_h5mu"
namespace: "Convert"
description: "Converts the output from Xenium to a single .h5mu file, where the count matrix is written to the `rna` modality."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `from_xenium_to_h5mu`  
Namespace: `convert`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu)

:::

The following files are expected to be present in the Xenium output bundle:
├── cell_feature_matrix.h5
├── cells.parquet
├── experiment.xenium
└── metrics_summary.csv



## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                   |Attributes                                          |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`              |Input folder. Must contain the output from a Xenium run.                                                      |`file`, required, example: `"xenium_output_bundle"` |
|`--output`             |Output .h5mu file.                                                                                            |`file`, example: `"xenium.h5mu"`                    |
|`--obsm_coordinates`   |Name of the .obsm slot under which to store the cell centroid coordinates.                                    |`string`, default: `"spatial"`                      |
|`--uns_experiment`     |Name of the .uns slot under which to store the Xenium experiment specifications.                              |`string`, default: `"xenium_experiment"`            |
|`--uns_metrics`        |Name of the .uns slot under which to store the summary QC metrics.                                            |`string`, default: `"xenium_metrics"`               |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata objects. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                         |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (maintainer)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
